ground_truth.title: Beginner Tutorials
ground_truth.main_content: MIT App Inventor is a free, cloud-based service that allows you to make your own mobile apps using a blocks-based programming language. You access App Inventor using a web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari). With these beginner-friendly tutorials, you will learn the basics of programming apps for Android and iOS phones and tablets. You will need a Mac or Windows computer and a Wi-Fi connection. You will make a mobile app, so it's fun to see it run on a phone or tablet while you build the app. Some setup is required to run your apps. Choose which way you want to connect to run your apps, and follow the links to setup instructions: Android or iOS device with Wi-Fi, Chromebook, Android device with USB cable, or on-screen emulator. If you hit any snags, help is available through the community support forum. We have four beginner tutorials to try out: Hello Codi!, Talk to Me (2 parts), Ball Bounce, and Digital Doodle. We recommend starting with Hello Codi!, but any of the four tutorials are accessible for novice coders. They can all be completed within an hour. For each tutorial, you can access them via the tutorial link to open a project in App Inventor, video links, or PDF text files. After building the starter apps, you can move on to extending them with more functionality, or start building apps of your own design. What's Next: Try turning TalkToMe into a Magic 8 Ball; turn BallBounce into a Mini Golf game, take DigitalDoodle to the next level by adding colors and incorporating pictures as backgrounds in PaintPot.
with: App Inventor is a free, cloud-based service that allows you to make your own mobile apps using a blocks-based programming language.
With these beginner-friendly tutorials, you will learn the basics of programming apps for Android and iOS phones and tablets.
We have four beginner tutorials to try out: Hello Codi!, Talk to Me (2 parts), Ball Bounce, and Digital Doodle.
Hello Codi! Get started by making Codi the Bee buzz when touched!
Ball Bounce: Fling, Flang, Flung? Whatever you call it, bouncing the ball around the screen is pure fun.
